prerequisites: A running Milvus instance (self-managed Milvus or Zilliz Cloud) with an accessible endpoint URL, and a target collection name for the indexing config.

Authentication enabled on the Milvus instance if you intend to authenticate (set common.security.authorizationEnabled to true on the Milvus instance); Milvus initializes a default root user with password Milvus.

An account with the embedding provider you choose in the embedding config: an OpenAI API key for OpenAI embeddings, or a Cohere API key for Cohere embeddings. A Fake embeddings option is available for testing without provider credentials.
Configuration values for the processing config, including the text fields to embed and the chunk size (max 8191 tokens for OpenAI-compatible embeddings).

authMethods.name: Milvus token (indexing.auth.token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to the Milvus instance with a token. Milvus uses token-based authentication where the token is the string username:password. The default root credentials produce the token root:Milvus until you create additional users.

authMethods.name: Milvus username and password (indexing.auth.password)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to the Milvus instance with an instance username and password instead of a single token. Passwords must be at least 6 and at most 256 characters. The default account is user root with password Milvus.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding API key (embedding.openai_key)
authMethods.summary: Provide an OpenAI API key when using the OpenAI embedding option, which embeds text with the text-embedding-ada-002 model (1536 dimensions).

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding API key (embedding.cohere_key)
authMethods.summary: Provide a Cohere API key when using the Cohere embedding option, which embeds text with the embed-english-light-v2.0 model (1024 dimensions).

authMethods.name: Embedding provider API key (embedding.api_key)
authMethods.summary: Provide the API key for the configured embedding provider. This is the generic embedding credential used by the embedding config alongside the provider-specific OpenAI and Cohere key fields.

setupSteps.title: Provision and reach a Milvus instance
setupSteps.body: Stand up a self-managed Milvus instance or a Zilliz Cloud instance and note its endpoint URL and the target collection name. You will supply both in the indexing config. If you require authenticated access, enable it by setting common.security.authorizationEnabled to true in the Milvus instance configuration.

setupSteps.title: Configure Milvus authentication in the indexing config
setupSteps.body: In the indexing config, supply the Milvus endpoint and collection, then provide credentials. Either set indexing.auth.token to a username:password token (the default is root:Milvus), or set the username and indexing.auth.password for username/password authentication.

setupSteps.title: Choose an embedding method and supply its key
setupSteps.body: In the embedding config, select an embedding method. For OpenAI set embedding.openai_key, for Cohere set embedding.cohere_key, or use the generic embedding.api_key for the configured provider. Use the Fake embeddings option to test the pipeline without provider credentials.

setupSteps.title: Configure processing and optional settings
setupSteps.body: In the processing config, specify the text fields to embed (dot notation and wildcards are supported), any metadata fields, and the chunk size in tokens (max 8191 for OpenAI-compatible embeddings). Optionally set omit_raw_text to avoid storing the raw source text alongside embeddings.
